title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: Apache Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework that allows for the distributed processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage. The framework provides a programming model that allows developers to write applications that process vast amounts of data in-parallel on large clusters. It achieves scalability, fault-tolerance, and maintainability by dividing the input data into smaller chunks, processing them in parallel, and aggregating the results. The framework uses a distributed file system (HDFS) to store data and a job tracker to schedule and coordinate the tasks across the cluster. It supports various programming models, including MapReduce, Pig, Hive, and Spark, and provides a rich set of libraries for data processing, such as MapReduce, HBase, and ZooKeeper.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Cluster 13
description: This cluster consists of classes related to output committers in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It includes the FileOutputCommitterFactory class, which creates a FileOutputCommitter, and the PathOutputCommitter class, which is a committer for data written to a working directory to the final directory during the commit process. The PathOutputCommitterFactory class is used to create committers for tasks in the Hadoop MapReduce framework. The cluster also includes the SequenceFileAsBinaryOutputFormat class, which writes keys and values to SequenceFile in binary(raw) format, and the SequenceFileOutputFormat class, which writes SequenceFile objects. The TextOutputFormat class is another component that provides an implementation of an OutputFormat that writes plain text files.

title: FileOutputFormat
description: The FileOutputFormat class is an abstract class that extends the OutputFormat class and provides functionality for writing data to a file system. It sets up the output committer for the job, checks the output specifications for the job, and gets the record writer for the job. The core logic of the FileOutputFormat class is to handle the output committer and record writer for the job. It also provides methods to set and get the compression settings for the output. The FileOutputFormat class takes inputs and outputs in the form of key-value pairs. The inputs are the job configuration and the task attempt context, while the outputs are the record writer and the output committer. The FileOutputFormat class has dependencies on other classes and packages such as OutputCommitter, TaskAttemptContext, Configuration, FileSystem, Path, and CompressionCodec.

title: MapReduce Cluster 14

The partitioner classes are used by the MapReduce framework to distribute data evenly across reduce tasks, which helps improve the performance and scalability of the system. The use of these partitioner classes ensures that the data is evenly distributed across the reduce tasks, which can improve the efficiency and scalability of the MapReduce job. The technologies and frameworks used in this cluster include Java, Hadoop, YARN, Avro, and Protocol Buffers. The role of this cluster in the overall MapReduce system is to ensure that the data is evenly distributed across the reduce tasks, which helps improve the performance and scalability of the system.
